title: Improving Health Care for Future Generations of Veterans
summary: VA’s Million Veteran Program (MVP) is paving the way for a future of personalized health care for all Veterans. By joining MVP, a community of 1 million+ Veterans, you can help make a difference for Veteran health care—now, and for future generations. Learn more and join today at https://www.mvp.va.gov or call 866-441-6075.

title: MVP Discoveries: Heart Health in People of African Ancestry
summary: VA’s Million Veteran Program (MVP) is making discoveries to improve Veteran health—like discovering a gene variant that raises the risk of dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM) that is twice as common in Black individuals as in white individuals. Help MVP make more discoveries for Veterans like you. To learn more or join today, visit https://www.mvp.va.gov or call 866-441-6075.

title: Have Racial Disparities in Law Enforcement Stops Narrowed?
summary: California’s Racial and Identity Profiling Act (RIPA) requires detailed reporting on all pedestrian and traffic stops. title: Policy Brief: Have Racial Disparities in Law Enforcement Stops Narrowed?

title: Beneath Norway's Mountains
summary: Above the Arctic Circle, winter dominates the landscape. Snow blankets the mountains and subzero winds cut across frozen valleys. For most people, this terrain can feel remote and inhospitable. However, for U.S. Marines alongside NATO Allies and partners, it is a proving ground. In the Arctic, the environment can be as lethal as any adversary. This reality makes joint readiness, training, and the ability to rapidly mobilize equipment and personnel to crisis not optional, but essential to operational success in the event of armed conflict.

summary:
This article was originally published by Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty and is reprinted with permission. When a Russian “nesting-doll” satellite maneuvered into close proximity to a US satellite last June, it was the latest move in a dangerous game of cat-and-mouse in space. Cosmos 2558 had been observed shadowing USA 326 ever since being launched in 2022. …

title: Video: Shots fired as Border Patrol aids state troopers in Texas ‘pursuit’
summary:
A video shows a Texas Department of Public Safety trooper firing shots at a moving vehicle after it allegedly rammed into a law enforcement vehicle during a “pursuit” that was “initiated” by the Texas Department of Public Safety and aided by U.S. Border Patrol agents on Saturday. title: Up to 5 sexually violent predators may be released in neighborhood with 7,400 kids
summary:

title: Democrat delegate exposed for connections to Epstein: Report
summary:
New documents released by the U.S. Justice Department allegedly expose Virgin Islands Delegate Stacey Plaskett, a Democrat, for being linked to Jeffrey Epstein, a convicted child sex offender and former financier who died in 2019. title: California Democratic Party and Contest Every Race Announce Statewide Candidate Recruitment Initiative
summary:
SACRAMENTO – On Saturday, January 31st, the California Democratic Party is launching a statewide texting campaign to recruit hundreds of Democrats to run for local office, with a focus in rural areas where GOP incumbents often run uncontested. This is CADEM’s fifth cycle participating in this national campaign by Contest Every Race to ensure that no […]

title: State Policymakers Discuss Priorities, Challenges for 2026
summary: Affordability is a top issue for policymakers from both parties, along with AI and immigration. Heath Flora, Republican leader of the California State Assembly, and Democratic Assemblymember José Luis Solache, Jr., discuss their concerns with PPIC president and CEO Tani Cantil-Sakauye.
